x-enumeration-literals.name: Principal Agreement Document
x-enumeration-literals.description: The core legal document that outlines the terms and obligations of the parties.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Annexes or Appendices
x-enumeration-literals.description:
These are documents attached to the main agreement to provide supporting details.

Examples:
Technical specifications
Pricing schedules
Product lists
Process diagrams
Organizational charts

x-enumeration-literals.name: Disclosure Schedules
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Often used in mergers and acquisitions (M&A), these schedules list exceptions or detailed information relevant to representations and warranties in the main agreement.

Examples:

List of contracts
Pending litigation
Employee details
Intellectual property owned

x-enumeration-literals.name: Confidentiality or Non-Disclosure Agreements (NDAs)
x-enumeration-literals.description: Separate agreements to protect sensitive information shared during negotiation or performance.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Amendments or Addendums
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Documents that modify or add to the original agreement, without replacing it.

Amendment: Alters terms (e.g., payment date)
Addendum: Adds new terms or clauses

x-enumeration-literals.name: Side Letters or Side Agreements
x-enumeration-literals.description: Agreements made outside but related to the main agreement, often clarifying or customizing certain provisions.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Consent or Waiver Letters
x-enumeration-literals.description: Formal acknowledgment where a party gives up a right or consents to a change, such as a deadline extension.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Due Diligence Documents
x-enumeration-literals.description:
In deals like acquisitions or partnerships, these documents support the evaluation of the other party.

Examples:

Financial statements
Tax filings
Employment contracts
Legal compliance reports

x-enumeration-literals.name: Termination Letters or Notices
x-enumeration-literals.description: Used to formally end or exit the agreement according to its terms.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Proof of Authority or Corporate Documents
x-enumeration-literals.description:
To show that a party has the legal power to sign or enforce the agreement.

Examples:

Board resolutions
Power of attorney
Certificates of incorporation
